# Out-of-Sample Future Hypervolume — paper headline metric

*Generated by `python_refactor/experiments/oos_report.py` (W13-3).
Implements thesis Eqs (7.10)+(7.11) — see [`docs/THESIS-INDEX.md`](THESIS-INDEX.md).*

## Protocol

- **In-sample window** (training): `paper`
- **Out-of-sample window** (evaluation): `paper[tail20%]`
- **Scenarios**: S0, S2
- **Seeds**: 30 (1..30)
- **MC scenarios per seed (E)**: 1000
- **Reference point z_ref**: (0.2, 0.0) — risk_max=0.2, return_min=0.0

## Results

| scenario | n_seeds | mean OOS EFHV | std | median | min | max |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| S0 | 30 | 5.12274e-05 | 4.0625e-06 | 5.12289e-05 | 4.56495e-05 | 6.02921e-05 |
| S2 | 30 | 4.22189e-05 | 3.68182e-06 | 4.27803e-05 | 3.27792e-05 | 4.86002e-05 |

## Headline observation (paper §V-D claim direction)

**S2 (anticipatory + max-Hypv DM = ASMS/mHDM) mean OOS EFHV = 4.22189e-05**
**S0 (myopic baseline = SMS/RDM) mean OOS EFHV = 5.12274e-05**

Delta (S2 − S0) = **-9.00851e-06** (-17.59%). Paper claim direction (Table 7.2): S2 > S0. Empirical direction here: **S2 ≤ S0 (inconsistent)**.
